title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Runtime and Build Recall
summary: An operations reviewer reconstructs images, layers, manifests, tags, digests, and registry identity from delayed evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and extends the image identity and layer provenance ledger under a changed constraint. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for image identity and layer provenance ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers · changed case · Review Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers
steps.instruction:
review case — A review release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.

Which action demonstrates Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer. For Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers · changed case · Review Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, keep Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.
